faq.question: Do I need an IDP to drive in Japan?
faq.question_de: Brauche ich in Japan einen IDP?
faq.answer: Yes for most foreign licences. Japan accepts IDPs under the 1949 Geneva Convention only—not 1968 Vienna. Some countries (Germany, Switzerland, France, etc.) need a Japanese translation instead. Check JAF before travel.
faq.answer_de: Ja für die meisten ausländischen Führerscheine. Japan akzeptiert IDP nur nach Genfer Konvention 1949—nicht Wien 1968. Manche Länder brauchen japanische Übersetzung. JAF vor Reise prüfen.

faq.question: Must I stop at railway crossings in Japan?
faq.question_de: Muss ich in Japan an Bahnübergängen anhalten?
faq.answer: Yes. You must stop completely before crossing, even if no train is visible. Violations carry heavy fines (¥70,000+) and licence points.
faq.answer_de: Ja. Vollständig anhalten vor Übergang, auch ohne sichtbaren Zug. Hohe Strafen (¥70.000+) und Punkte.

faq.question: Can I turn right on red in Japan?
faq.question_de: Darf ich in Japan bei Rot rechts abbiegen?
faq.answer: No. Turning on red is not permitted unless a green arrow specifically allows it. Japan drives on the left—'easy' right turns still require full signal compliance.
faq.answer_de: Nein. Abbiegen bei Rot nicht erlaubt, außer grüner Pfeil erlaubt es. Linksverkehr—auch 'einfache' Rechtsabbieger brauchen grünes Signal.

faq.question: How do expressway tolls work in Japan?
faq.answer: Most expressways (kosoku-doro) are tolled. Take ticket at entry, pay at exit. ETC card speeds payment—rentals often include ETC. NEXCO operates most routes.
faq.answer_de: Die meisten Schnellstraßen sind mautpflichtig. Ticket bei Einfahrt, Bezahlung bei Ausfahrt. ETC-Karte beschleunigt Zahlung—Mietwagen oft mit ETC.

faq.question: What is the alcohol limit in Japan?
faq.answer: Legal limit 0.03% BAC (0.15 mg/L breath). Penalties are severe; passengers who supplied alcohol to a drunk driver can also be fined. Zero tolerance culturally—do not drink and drive.
faq.answer_de: Gesetzlich 0,03 % BAC. Strenge Strafen; Mitfahrer die Alkohol bereitstellen können auch bestraft werden. Kulturell null Toleranz.

faq.question: What does the Japanese stop sign mean?
faq.answer: Red inverted triangle with 止まれ (tomare). Full stop required—rolling stops are fined. More common than in Europe.
faq.answer_de: Rotes Dreieck mit 止まれ (tomare). Vollständiger Stopp Pflicht—Rollstopps werden geahndet.

faq.question: Winter driving in Hokkaido?
faq.answer: Winter tyres mandatory Oct–Apr. Black ice common. Deer on roads at night. Lower speed limits on many Hokkaido expressways (80 km/h).
faq.answer_de: Winterreifen Okt–Apr Pflicht. Glatteis häufig. Hirsche nachts. Niedrigere Limits auf Hokkaido-Autobahnen.

summary: Drive on the Left. Right turns feel 'easy', left turns need attention. Speed limits are generally lower than many countries and strictly enforced. Expect toll roads on established expressways. Keep an eye out for railway crossings (must stop before crossing) and pedestrian priority.
common_traps: Cycling laws are strict; watch for cyclists.

speed_limits.notes: Signposted limits override defaults. Variable speed limits on expressways are common.

alcohol_limit.notes: Strict Zero Tolerance in practice. Penalties are severe for both driver and passengers.

rules.child_seat_rules: Required for children under 6 years old.
rules.phone_usage_rules: Handheld usage prohibited. Hands-free allowed but restricted.
rules.headlights_rules: Must be on at night and in tunnels. High beams recommended on unlit roads.
rules.priority_rules: Pedestrians have right of way. Stop for school buses.

tolls.notes: Most expressways are toll roads. ETC card recommended for rentals.

rules.parking_rules: Strictly enforced. Use designated parking lots (Coin Parking). Street parking often prohibited.
rules.winter_rules: Winter tires mandatory in snowy regions (Hokkaido, Tohoku, etc.)

motorcycle.helmet_rules: Usage of helmets is mandatory for both rider and passenger.
motorcycle.lane_splitting_rules: Lane splitting (filtering) is technically not illegal if done safely, but police may ticket for crossing lines. Exercise caution.
motorcycle.overtaking_rules: Overtake only where lane markings and visibility allow it. Do not pass in intersections, near crossings, or where stopping/priority rules apply.
motorcycle.city_traffic_rules: Urban traffic is dense and pedestrian priority is strict. Watch for bicycles, taxis, buses, and narrow lanes.
motorcycle.motorway_access: Allowed for motorcycles over 125cc. Tandem riding on highways requires >1 year license + >20 years old.
motorcycle.parking_rules: Must use designated motorcycle parking. Car spots often prohibited.

speed_limits.notes: No motorway access. Small mopeds are slower than cars; local class limits can be as low as 30 km/h and posted limits may be lower.

moped.lane_splitting_rules: Filtering is risky and can be ticketed if it involves unsafe lane crossing or obstruction.
moped.overtaking_rules: Keep left and overtake only when clearly permitted and safe. Do not use expressways.
moped.city_traffic_rules: Two-step right turns may be required at large intersections. Watch bicycle lanes and narrow urban streets.
moped.licensing_notes: Car license usually includes 50cc moped privileges.

rental_and_idp_notes: International Driving Permit (1949 Convention) required. Some licenses (Swiss, German, French, etc.) need a translation.

regional_variations.notes: Parking is extremely expensive and strict. Use public transport where possible. 'Shuto Expressway' is complex and narrow.
Drivers are known to be more aggressive than in Tokyo. Running yellow lights is common locally (Osaka-bashiri).

speed_limits.notes: Wide roads often lead to speeding, but enforcement is high. Winter tires mandatory Oct-Apr.

regional_variations.notes: Roads are wider and straighter. Watch for wildlife (deer/foxes). severe winter conditions.

road_signs.title: Stop
road_signs.description: Triangle pointing down. Text: 止まれ (Tomare). Must stop completely.

road_signs.title: No Entry
road_signs.description: Red circle with white horizontal bar.

road_signs.title: Slow Down
road_signs.description: Triangle pointing down. Text: 徐行 (Jokō). Drive slowly (immediately stoppable).

summaries.title: Speeding
summaries.summary: Lower limits than Europe; strict enforcement with cameras.
summaries.summary_de: Niedrigere Limits als Europa; strenge Kamera-Kontrolle.

summaries.title: Railway crossings
summaries.summary: Full stop mandatory before every crossing.
summaries.summary_de: Vollständiger Stopp vor jedem Übergang Pflicht.
